summary: Soaring Dark Star, tight Hard To Handle, vibrant 1970 set
blurb: The show delivers a soaring Dark Star and a punchy Hard To Handle, with Phil’s vocal on point and Jerry’s tone glowing. The second half builds into jam‑heavy passages highlighted by The Other One, Good Lovin’ and a classic closing stretch.
review: On May 24 1970 the Dead opened their first English festival set in Newcastle with a mix of tight rockers and expansive jams that still feels fresh today. Jerry’s guitar shines on Dark Star – reviewers call it “breathtaking” and note his new Gibson SG tone – and he pushes the energy forward on Hard To Handle. Phil’s vocals on the ballads are described as loving and vulnerable, while Bob anchors the groove with a solid Me And My Uncle and reliable rhythm work. The second half rolls through The Other One into an uplifting Good Lovin’/Cold Rain And Snow double‑take before launching into a soaring Dark Star that segues into Saint Stephen and a rousing finale of Not Fade Away and Turn On Your Lovelight. Across all tapes the performance is uniformly praised, with the classic jam “And We Bid You Goodnight” cited as a highlight in one board capture, underscoring just how powerful this early‑‘70 European outing was.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings are marked positive; multiple reviewers single out Dark Star, Hard To Handle, Phil’s vocals and the classic jam as exceptional. No negative comments appear, so a strong positive sentiment adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, balanced mix; highlights Dark Star and Hard To Handle while preserving the full set’s energy.

key_highlights: Soaring Dark Star that many call breathtaking
Punchy Hard To Handle driving the early set
Phil’s vocal performance praised as loving and vulnerable
Jam‑heavy second half featuring The Other One, Good Lovin’ and classic closing jam

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone grooves on Dark Star and he picks up the pace during Hard To Handle, delivering a breathtaking solo that hints at his new Gibson SG sound.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s vocals are highlighted as loving and vulnerable, adding emotional depth to the ballads and harmonies.
band_performance.Bob: Weir provides solid rhythm work and delivers a nice Me And My Uncle, keeping the groove tight throughout.
